text: A particle executes simple harmonic motion with amplitude A. The distance from the mean position where its kinetic energy is equal to potential energy is
explanation: In SHM, total energy \(E = \frac{1}{2} m \omega^2 A^2\). At displacement \(x\), KE = \(E - \frac{1}{2} m \omega^2 x^2\), PE = \(\frac{1}{2} m \omega^2 x^2\). Set KE = PE: \(E - \frac{1}{2} m \omega^2 x^2 = \frac{1}{2} m \omega^2 x^2\). Solving gives \(x = A / \sqrt{2}\).
name: Questions Item 1

text: The period of oscillation of a simple pendulum of length L suspended from the roof of a vehicle which moves without friction down an inclined plane of inclination \(\alpha\) is
explanation: Effective acceleration due to gravity = \(g \cos \alpha\) (component along the string). Time period \(T = 2\pi \sqrt{L / g_{eff}} = 2\pi \sqrt{L / (g \cos \alpha)}\).
name: Questions Item 2

text: A particle executes simple harmonic motion with an amplitude of 4 cm. At what displacement from the mean position is its kinetic energy equal to its potential energy?
explanation: Set KE = PE: \(x = A / \sqrt{2} = 4 / \sqrt{2} = 2\sqrt{2}\) cm.
name: Questions Item 3

text: A particle executes SHM with amplitude A and time period T. The time required for the particle to travel from \(x = A/2\) to \(x = A\) is
explanation: From \(x = A \cos \omega t\), time from \(x = A/2\) to \(x = A\) corresponds to phase change from \(\pi/3\) to 0. Time = \((\pi/3) / \omega = T/6\).
name: Questions Item 4

text: The displacement of a particle executing SHM is given by \(x = 0.5 \sin (100 t + \phi)\). The maximum velocity of the particle is
explanation: Velocity \(v = dx/dt = 50 \cos (100 t + \phi)\). Maximum velocity = 50 ms^{-1}.
name: Questions Item 5

text: A simple pendulum of length l is suspended from the ceiling of a cart which is sliding down on an inclined plane of inclination \(\theta\). The time period of the pendulum is
explanation: Effective g = g (1 + sin θ). Hence T = 2π √(l / g(1 + sin θ)).
name: Questions Item 6

text: The time period of a simple pendulum is T. If the length of the pendulum is increased by 44%, the percentage increase in time period is
explanation: T ∝ √l. New l' = 1.44 l, new T' = T √1.44 = 1.2 T. Percentage increase = 20%.
name: Questions Item 7

text: A particle is executing SHM with amplitude A and angular frequency ω. The ratio of its maximum acceleration to its maximum velocity is
explanation: Maximum acceleration = ω² A, maximum velocity = ω A. Ratio = ω.
name: Questions Item 8

text: The displacement-time graph of a particle executing SHM is shown in figure. The time period of the oscillation is
explanation: From graph, one full cycle (from mean to mean) takes 8 s.
name: Questions Item 9

text: A particle executes SHM with amplitude 4 cm. At what displacement from mean position is the kinetic energy equal to potential energy?
explanation: KE = PE when x = A/√2 = 4/√2 = 2√2 cm.
name: Questions Item 10

text: The phase difference between displacement and velocity of a particle executing SHM is
explanation: Velocity leads displacement by π/2 in SHM.
name: Questions Item 11

text: The potential energy of a simple harmonic oscillator is maximum when the displacement from mean position is
explanation: PE = (1/2) m ω² x², maximum at x = A.
name: Questions Item 12

text: A particle of mass m executes SHM with amplitude A and frequency f. The total energy of the particle is
explanation: Total energy E = 2 π² m f² A².
name: Questions Item 13

text: The time period of a simple pendulum in a lift accelerating upwards with acceleration g/3 is
explanation: Effective g = g + g/3 = 4g/3. T = 2π √(l / (4g/3)).
name: Questions Item 14

text: A particle executes SHM. The ratio of its maximum velocity to maximum acceleration is
explanation: v_max = ω A, a_max = ω² A. Ratio v_max / a_max = 1/ω.
name: Questions Item 15

text: The time period of oscillation of a simple pendulum of length l suspended from the roof of a vehicle moving without friction down an inclined plane of inclination α is
explanation: Effective g = g cos α. Hence T = 2π √(l / (g cos α)).
name: Questions Item 16

text: The equation of motion of a particle executing SHM is x = 0.25 sin (200 t + φ). The maximum velocity of the particle is
name: Questions Item 17

text: A simple pendulum has time period T. If its length is doubled and acceleration due to gravity is halved, the new time period is
explanation: New T' = 2π √(2l / (g/2)) = 2π √(4 l/g) = 2 T_original? Wait, standard: √(4) = 2, but calculation gives √2 T.
name: Questions Item 18

text: The velocity of a particle executing SHM at the mean position is
explanation: At mean position, v = ω A (maximum velocity).
name: Questions Item 19

text: The total energy of a particle executing SHM is 0.5 J. If its amplitude is doubled, the new total energy will be
explanation: E ∝ A². New E = 0.5 × (2)² = 2 J.
name: Questions Item 20

text: The time period of a mass suspended from a spring is T. If the spring is cut into four equal parts and the same mass is suspended from one of them, the new time period is
explanation: New k' = 4k. New T' = 2π √(m / 4k) = T/2.
name: Questions Item 21

text: A particle executes SHM with frequency f. The frequency of oscillation of its kinetic energy is
explanation: KE varies as cos²(ωt), which has frequency 2f.
name: Questions Item 22

text: A body of mass 0.5 kg executes SHM with amplitude 0.2 m and frequency 4 Hz. The maximum force acting on the body is
name: Questions Item 23

text: The time period of a simple pendulum of length l is T. If the length is increased to 4l, the new time period is
explanation: T ∝ √l. New T' = T √4 = 2T.
name: Questions Item 24

text: A particle executes SHM. The ratio of its maximum velocity to maximum acceleration is
explanation: v_max / a_max = (ω A) / (ω² A) = 1/ω.
name: Questions Item 25
